rationale: Both correctly endorse closing as not-reproducible, but the thread explicitly states the maintainer had no confirmed explanation and suspected a contaminated session (hot patches, shared GPU) — X stays faithful to that uncertainty, while Y confidently constructs an unconfirmed mechanistic theory ('explains perfectly', 'smoking gun') about EOS-logit depression from stale cudagraph buffers that goes beyond what anyone established. Y is more grounded (specific config/line citations) and more complete (concrete PIECEWISE-before-enforce_eager diagnostic path), but its overreach on causal certainty is a real calibration miss against the actual thread resolution.
